title: Complete Airport Arrival Guide
summary: The full process from landing to clearing customs, including immigration, luggage, and pickup arrangements.

content.text: Arriving at a US airport for the first time can feel overwhelming, but the process is straightforward. This guide walks you through every step.

items.title: Follow the signs after landing
items.description: Follow 'Arrivals' or 'Immigration' signs to the inspection area.

items.title: Wait in line for immigration
items.description: Join the 'Non-US Citizens' or 'Visitors' line. Have your passport, I-20, and I-94 (usually electronic) ready.

items.title: Immigration officer interview
items.description: Answer simple questions: purpose of visit (study), school name, length of stay. Be polite and confident.

items.title: Collect your luggage
items.description: Find your flight number at the baggage carousel and wait for your bags. If luggage is lost, report to the Baggage Claim counter.

items.title: Go through customs
items.description: If you have nothing to declare, take the green lane. Cash over $10,000 or food items must be declared.

items.title: Reach the arrivals area
items.description: After clearing customs, you'll enter the Arrivals hall. Find your ride or transportation.

content.text: Do not bring meat, fruit, seeds, or other agricultural products into the US. Getting caught means fines. If unsure, declare it honestly on the customs form.
Screenshot important info on your phone before landing: pickup contact number, housing address, school international office phone. You can view these even without internet.
